title: 10 best herbal capsules for periodontal disease

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander capsules helps with periodontal disease because they contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum inflammation and infection. The capsules' potent anti-inflammatory properties also reduce swelling and pain in the gums, making it an effective natural treatment for periodontitis. Additionally, germander has been found to stimulate bone formation, which can help rebuild damaged tissue and prevent tooth loss.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Germander capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, reducing the risk of periodontal disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in germander capsules can help reduce swelling, pain, and redness associated with periodontal disease, promoting a healthier mouth environment.

Antimicrobial properties: Germander capsules contain antimicrobial agents that can inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, reducing the risk of infection.
remedy_constituents: Diterpenes: Diterpenes present in Teucrium chamaedrys have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat bacteria and reduce inflammation associated with periodontal disease.

Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): Flavonoids in Teucrium chamaedrys have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect oral tissues from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by periodontal disease.

Rosmarinlic acid: Rosmarinlic acid, a phenolic diterpene, has been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential candidate to combat periodontal pathogens and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Teucrium chamaedrys are likely used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, which may contribute to their potential therapeutic properties.
Roots: The roots of the plant might be used for their ability to absorb and store nutrients, which could be beneficial in addressing periodontal disease.
Stems: The stems of Teucrium chamaedrys might be used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and other compounds that could potentially aid in the treatment of periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh dried teucrium chamaedrys leaves in late summer when they are most potent.
Clean the leaves thoroughly with water to remove dirt and debris then dry them.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or grinder.
Mix the powder with 50g of gelatin and 20g of glycerin in a glass bowl then stir well.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the powder mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with periodontal disease because of its potent antibacterial properties, which can help combat the underlying infections that contribute to gum disease. The capsule's active ingredients, such as rosmarinic acid and apigenin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria like Porphyromonas gingivalis and Fusobacterium nucleatum, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the gums. This natural approach can be a valuable adjunct to conventional treatments, helping to alleviate symptoms and prevent further damage.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Lemon balm's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria such as Porphyromonas gingivalis and Fusobacterium nucleatum, which are common causes of periodontal disease, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The rosmarinic acid present in lemon balm has been shown to exhibit potent anti-inflammatory activity, which helps alleviate the swelling, redness, and pain associated with periodontal disease by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals like prostaglandins and leukotrienes.

Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm's high antioxidant content, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, helps protect against oxidative stress and tissue damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to periodontal disease progression and tooth loss.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound has antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the growth of bacteria associated with periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis and Aggregatibacter actinomycetemcomitans.

Luteolin: As a flavonoid, luteolin exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate the inflammation and oxidative stress that contribute to periodontal disease progression.

Oleanolic acid: This triterpenoid saponin has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria and reduce the inflammation associated with periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat periodontal disease.
Roots: The roots are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in periodontal tissues.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that contribute to periodontal disease, thereby promoting oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated when flowers are in full bloom.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 4 grams of dried melissa officinalis plant material into a fine powder using a mill or blender.
Mix the powdered plant material with 2 grams of gelatin or vegetarian alternative in a bowl to create a uniform mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal immediately to preserve the herbal extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with periodontal disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation. The capsule's antimicrobial properties also work to combat the bacteria that cause periodontitis, promoting a healthy balance of oral flora. Additionally, pot marigold contains flavonoids and carotenoids that help to strengthen gum tissue, improving overall oral health and reducing the risk of tooth loss.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold's antioxidant compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, which can contribute to periodontal disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal extracts in pot marigold capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, reducing swelling and pain associated with periodontitis.

Antibacterial properties: Pot marigold's antibacterial compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help combat the bacterial growth that can lead to periodontal disease by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and promoting healthy oral flora.
remedy_constituents: Naphthoquinones: These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce bacterial growth and inflammation in periodontal disease.
Sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in periodontal disease.

Phenolic acids (such as rutin and quercetin): These compounds have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce bacterial growth, inflammation, and oxidative stress in periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the primary used part due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help combat periodontal disease.
Leaves: Leaves are also used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, contributing to the management of periodontal disease.
Seeds: The seeds of Calendula officinalis are used for their antiseptic and antiviral properties, which aid in preventing infections and promoting oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh flowers of calendula officinalis when in full bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 1 to 3 weeks.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Mix the calendula powder with a small amount of inert filler and a binding agent to create a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the uniform blend and seal according to manufacturer instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage capsules helps with periodontal disease because they contain antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial growth, reducing inflammation and preventing further damage to gum tissues. The antioxidant compounds in sage also help protect against oxidative stress and tissue destruction, promoting healthy gums and bone density. Additionally, sage's anti-inflammatory effects help reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease, providing relief for patients suffering from this condition.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: Herbal sage capsules contain antimicrobial compounds like thymol, which help combat bacterial growth and reduce inflammation in the gums, thereby preventing further destruction of the gum tissue.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in sage capsules can help neutralize free radicals that damage gums and teeth, thereby protecting the oral cavity from further damage and promoting overall health.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the oral cavity, thus mitigating periodontal disease progression.
Ursolic acid: A triterpenoid that exhibits antimicrobial properties, which can inhibit the growth of pathogenic bacteria responsible for periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis.
Caryophyllene oxide: A sesquiterpene that has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce periodontal inflammation and promote tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat periodontal disease.
Roots: Utilized for their ability to reduce inflammation and fight against bacterial growth associated with periodontal disease.
Stems: Employed for their potential anti-inflammatory properties and ability to promote wound healing in periodontal conditions.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 pound of dried salvia officinalis leaves and stems that are harvested during the flowering stage.
Crush 10 grams of the dried salvia officinalis material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with 1 tablespoon of gelatin and 1 tablespoon of glycerin to form a homogeneous mixture.
Fill 100 empty capsules with 0.2 grams of the powder mixture each and seal them properly.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mongolian dandelion capsules helps with periodontal disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules contain extracts rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum inflammation and infection. Additionally, Mongolian dandelion's antioxidant capabilities help to reduce oxidative stress and promote healing in the gums, preventing further tissue damage. This natural remedy has been traditionally used to combat periodontal disease, offering a safe and effective alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Mongolian Dandelion Capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, which can contribute to periodontal disease.
Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The capsules' anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease by suppressing pro-inflammatory cytokines and eicosanoids.

Immune System Modulation: Mongolian Dandelion Capsules have been shown to stimulate the immune system's natural defense mechanisms, which can help combat bacterial infections and other underlying causes of periodontal disease.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help with periodontal disease by exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce the growth of bacteria and inflammation in the gums.

Flavonoids: They help with periodontal disease by displaying antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, promoting healing and reducing tissue damage.

Apolyphenols: They help with periodontal disease by showing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, promoting the healing of periodontal tissues and reducing the progression of periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: Used for their potential to inhibit the growth of bacteria and reduce inflammation in the gums.
Seeds: Used as they contain compounds that may have antibacterial and antioxidant effects, beneficial in treating periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Collect 250 grams of dried taraxacum mongolicum root in a clean environment to prevent contamination.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Mix 50 grams of the powder with 100 grams of gelatin in a bowl for encapsulation.
Fill each capsule shell with 0.5 grams of the gelatin mixture using a capsule filler for 30 minutes.
Store the herbal taraxacum mongolicum capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with periodontal disease because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation. The capsules also boost the body's natural defenses, enhancing the immune system's ability to fight off bacterial infections that contribute to periodontitis. Additionally, aloe's antioxidant properties help protect gums from oxidative damage, promoting healthy tissue growth and reducing the risk of tooth loss. As a result, herbal aloe capsules offer a natural solution for managing periodontal disease symptoms and preventing further complications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, aloe-emodin, and chromone, which can reduce inflammation in the gums and mouth, alleviating pain and swelling associated with periodontal disease.

Antibacterial properties: Herbal aloe capsules have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Streptococcus mutans and Escherichia coli, which are commonly found in plaque and contribute to periodontal disease.

Wound-healing properties: Aloe vera's gel contains enzymes like protease and alkaline phosphatase, which promote wound healing by accelerating tissue repair and regeneration, helping to restore damaged gums and bone tissue.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: A glycoside found in Aloe vera, Aloin has anti-inflammatory properties which help reduce gum inflammation and alleviate periodontal disease symptoms.
Aloe-emodin: A phenolic compound with known antimicrobial properties, Aloe-emodin helps combat the bacterial infections associated with periodontal disease by inhibiting the growth of oral pathogens.

Glucomannan: A polysaccharide with prebiotic properties, Glucomannan promotes a healthy oral microbiome, reducing the risk of periodontal disease by supporting the growth of beneficial bacteria and preventing overgrowth of pathogenic bacteria.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe vera for medicinal purposes, including periodontal disease, due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds.

Gel (from Leaves): The clear gel inside the Aloe vera leaf is rich in vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants, making it effective in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing associated with periodontal disease.

Aloin (from Leaves): Aloin, a compound found in the leaves of Aloe vera, has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms of periodontal disease, such as pain, swelling, and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100-200 mature aloe vera leaves with thick green skin and store them in a cool dry place for up to 5 days.
Peel the leaves and extract 200-300ml of aloe vera gel using a juicer or blender within 30 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of glycerin and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the gel and mix well for 5 minutes.
Fill a capsule machine with 0.5-1 gram of the gel mixture per capsule and compress 100-200 capsules in 10 minutes.
Store the herbal aloe vera capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with periodontal disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria that contribute to periodontitis, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis. Additionally, licorice's antimicrobial properties help to combat other oral pathogens, promoting a healthy balance of flora in the mouth and supporting gum tissue recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal licorice capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums and bones, alleviating symptoms of periodontitis.

Antimicrobial properties: Licorice root has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, helping to prevent infection and promote healing.

Glycoproteinase inhibition: The compound glycyrrhizin in licorice root has been found to inhibit the activity of glycoproteases, enzymes involved in bone resorption, which can help reduce the breakdown of jawbone tissue and stabilize teeth.
remedy_constituents: Licoricidin: Licoricidin has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth in the gums, thereby aiding in the management of periodontal disease.

Isoliquiritigenin: Isoliquiritigenin exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, thus promoting healing and preventing further damage to the periodontal tissues.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help to reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response, thereby preventing the progression of periodontal disease and promoting a healthy periodontal environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help combat periodontal disease.
Barks: Utilized for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the gums.
Leaves: Employed for their antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, which help protect the gums from damage and infection, thereby treating periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra, ensuring they are free from contaminants and moisture.
Grind the harvested roots into a fine powder using a mill or blender for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the powder with 10 grams of vegetable cellulose powder in a bowl.
Fill empty capsules with the powder mixture, following the manufacturer's guidelines for filling capacity.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dry place for up to 2 years.

remedy_properties: Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have been shown to exhibit antibacterial properties, which can help combat the bacteria that cause periodontal disease.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, including luteolin and apigenin, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in periodontal tissue, thereby reducing symptoms of periodontal disease.

Terpenes: Terpenes, such as sesquiterpenes and triterpenes, have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help combat infection and inflammation associated with periodontal disease.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with periodontal disease because they contain a potent anti-inflammatory compound called ursolic acid. This natural extract has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can contribute to periodontal disease, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy gums. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of Kansas coneflower help protect against oxidative stress, which can damage gum tissue and exacerbate symptoms of periodontal disease. By targeting the underlying causes of the condition, herbal Kansas coneflower capsules provide a natural solution for managing periodontal disease.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the growth of periodontal pathogens and prevent the development of periodontal disease.

Alkaloids (Cichorine, Angustifolin): The alkaloids present in Echinacea angustifolia have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response in periodontal tissues.

Flavonoids (Quercetin, Kaempferol): Flavonoids in Echinacea angustifolia have been reported to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect periodontal tissues from oxidative damage and reduce inflammation associated with periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of alkylamides and phenolic compounds, which are believed to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: They are used because of their high concentration of polysaccharides, which can stimulate the immune system and help fight off infections associated with periodontal disease.
Seeds: They are used due to their content of alkylamides, which have been shown to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects that may help alleviate symptoms of periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-50 grams of fresh echinacea angustifolia roots and clean them thoroughly in cold water.
Dry the roots in a warm air circulation dryer at 50-60 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours.
Grind 20-30 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using a mill grinder.
Mix the powder with 100-200 grams of gelatin capsules in a ratio of 1:5.
Fill the capsules with the powder mixture using an automatic capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek capsules helps with periodontal disease because they contain bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds, such as galactomannans and saponins, help to reduce inflammation in the gums, which is a key factor in periodontal disease. Additionally, fenugreek has been shown to stimulate collagen synthesis, promoting healthy gum tissue and bone growth. By reducing inflammation and promoting tissue health, fenugreek capsules can help to prevent further damage and promote healing in patients with periodontal disease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fenugreek's antioxidant compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with periodontal disease, preventing tissue damage and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in fenugreek capsules, such as galactomannan and saponins, inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, reducing swelling and pain in the gums and mouth.

Antibacterial properties: Fenugreek's antibacterial properties help combat the growth of bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, reducing the risk of tooth loss and improving overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds have antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that contribute to periodontal disease.

Phenolic acids (including ferulic acid and caffeic acid): These compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to mitigate the damage caused by oxidative stress and inflammation in periodontal disease.

Isoflavones (including genistein and daidzein): These compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce the severity of periodontal disease and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Trigonella foenum graecum are primarily used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, particularly saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties beneficial in treating periodontal disease.

Leaves: The leaves are used due to their rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been demonstrated to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to the management of periodontal disease.

Seeds: The seeds are also used due to their high saponin content, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of periodontal pathogens, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing in the context of periodontal disease treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried trigonella foenum graecum flowers from a reliable source.
Mill the dried flowers into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 30 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the milled powder with 20 grams of gelatin in a bowl.
Add 50 milliliters of distilled water to the mixture and stir gently for 10 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately using a capsule filler.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon capsules helps with periodontal disease because they contain a powerful antioxidant compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. This active compound targets and reduces inflammation in the gums, thereby preventing the progression of periodontal disease. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacteria that can contribute to gum disease, promoting healthy gums and teeth. Regular use of these capsules may even reverse mild cases of periodontitis.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Cinnamon has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis and Prevotella intermedia, helping to prevent infection and reduce inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory property: Cinnamon's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, help to reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontitis, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further damage.
Antioxidant property: Cinnamon is rich in antioxidants that help protect gum tissue from oxidative stress caused by free radicals, which can contribute to periodontal disease progression.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: A primary component of cinnamon oil, cinnamaldehyde has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat the bacterial infection and inflammation associated with periodontal disease.

Eugenol: A phenolic compound found in cinnamon bark, eugenol has been demonstrated to have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce the growth of periodontal pathogens and protect against oxidative stress.

Coumarin: A lignan present in cinnamon bark, coumarin has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative damage in the periodontal tissues.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help reduce plaque and prevent gum inflammation.
Leaves: Used because of their antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth in the gums.
Rhyzomes: Used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth in the gums, helping to prevent periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 milligrams of cinnamomum verum bark powder and place it in a bowl for later use.
Mix 250 milligrams of the cinnamomum verum powder with 250 milligrams of rice flour in a bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill gelatin capsules with the cinnamomum verum and rice flour mixture.
Label and package the filled capsules in an airtight container for storage.
Store the capsules in a cool dry place for up to two years for optimal potency preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with periodontal disease because of their potent anti-inflammatory properties. Curcumin, a key compound in turmeric, has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to periodontitis. By inhibiting enzymes that promote inflammatory responses, curcumin helps to alleviate symptoms such as swelling, redness, and pain associated with gum disease. Additionally, turmeric's antimicrobial properties may also help combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate periodontal disease, promoting overall oral health and reducing the risk of tooth loss.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's anti-inflammatory effects: Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with periodontal disease.

Antibacterial properties: Turmeric contains curcuminoids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria associated with periodontitis, including Porphyromonas gingivalis and Aggregatibacter actinomycetemcomitans.

Wound-healing promotion: Curcumin has been found to promote wound healing by enhancing collagen synthesis, improving blood flow, and reducing oxidative stress, which can help repair damaged gum tissue and bone.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in periodontal tissue.

Roots: Contain bioactive compounds like kaempferol and quercetin, which have antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, helping to combat bacterial growth and oxidative stress associated with periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of dried turmeric rhizomes curcuma longa for grinding into a fine powder.
Measure out 100g of the turmeric powder and mix it with 50g of silica gel for 10 minutes.
Sift the mixture through a 200-mesh sieve to ensure uniform particle size and remove any lumps.
Fill hard gelatin capsules with 500mg of the sifted mixture and seal them immediately using an encapsulation machine.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container for up to 12 months at room temperature or refrigerate them for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules help with periodontal disease because they contain bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, work by reducing inflammation in the gums and slowing down the progression of bone loss. Additionally, ginger has been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria associated with periodontal disease, thereby preventing further damage to the teeth and surrounding tissues. By taking herbal ginger capsules regularly, individuals can help alleviate symptoms of periodontal disease and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant property: Ginger's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to periodontal disease by protecting against damage caused by free radicals.

Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, reducing swelling and pain associated with periodontitis.

Immune-boosting property: Ginger's immunomodulatory effects can help stimulate the body's natural defense against bacterial infections, including those that contribute to periodontal disease, by enhancing the activity of immune cells such as neutrophils and macrophages.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce gum inflammation and prevent tissue damage.

Roots: The roots of the ginger plant contain a high concentration of gingerol, a compound known for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, making it effective in treating periodontal infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh zingiber officinale roots and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm place away from direct sunlight for 3-5 days.
Grind 10g of dried ginger into a fine powder using an electric spice grinder or mortar pestle.
Mix the powdered ginger with 50mg of vitamin c and 20ml of distilled water to form a paste.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with approximately 500mg of the prepared paste mixture and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram capsules helps with periodontal disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, a powerful antioxidant that has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, thereby preventing further tissue damage. Additionally, sweet marjoram's antimicrobial properties help to combat harmful bacteria in the mouth, promoting healthy gum growth and reducing symptoms of periodontal disease such as bleeding, swelling, and bad breath.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Sweet marjoram's antibacterial compounds, such as carvacrol and thymol, can help combat bacterial infections in the mouth, reducing inflammation and preventing further damage to gums and teeth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marjoram's anti-inflammatory compounds, including beta-caryophyllene and linalool, can reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontal disease, promoting healing and comfort.

Antioxidant properties: Sweet marjoram's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that contribute to tissue damage and chronic inflammation in the mouth, supporting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help combat inflammation and bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease.
Barks: Contain tannins, which have antibacterial properties that can help reduce plaque and prevent infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum majorana leaves and flowers in the morning when they are dry and fragrant.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation at room temperature for seven days.
Grind 20 grams of dried origanum majorana into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered origanum majorana with 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol and let it steep for three hours.
Filter the liquid extract through cheesecloth and fill gelatin capsules with one half teaspoon of the extract powder each.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Creeping thyme capsules helps with periodontal disease because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the gums. The antimicrobial properties of creeping thyme also inhibit the growth of bacteria that can contribute to gum disease, promoting a healthy balance of oral flora. Additionally, the capsule's soothing effects may help to alleviate gum pain and discomfort associated with periodontal disease, allowing for faster healing and improved overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thymol, a compound found in thyme, has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Streptococcus mutans and Porphyromonas gingivalis, which are responsible for periodontal disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's flavonoids, such as luteolin and apigenin, have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums and periodontal tissues associated with periodontal disease.

Antioxidant properties: Thyme contains antioxidants like thymol and carvacrol that can help protect against oxidative stress and damage to gum tissue, which can contribute to periodontal disease progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of creeping thyme contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antibacterial properties, making them effective in combating periodontal disease.

Rhyzomes: Creeping thyme's rhyzomes are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums and surrounding tissues, promoting healthy oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus serpyllum flowers and leaves in their peak blooming season.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade or sun drying methods.
Grind the dried thymus serpyllum into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of the powdered thymus serpyllum with 500mg of silica filler and 20mg of magnesium stearate in a bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with the blended mixture and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi capsules helps with periodontal disease because they contain Bacopa monnieri, a natural herb that has anti-inflammatory properties. This herb is known to reduce inflammation in the gums and tissues surrounding the teeth, thereby helping to alleviate symptoms of periodontal disease such as redness, swelling, and bleeding. Additionally, Brahmi capsules have antioxidant properties that help to combat free radicals and promote healthy gum tissue, reducing the risk of further damage and promoting a healthy oral environment.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Brahmi capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, which can contribute to periodontal disease.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herbal compounds in Brahmi capsules have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontitis, a common condition characterized by gum infection.
Wound-healing properties: Brahmi's wound-healing properties can aid in the repair of damaged gum tissue and bone caused by periodontal disease, promoting healthy gum attachment to teeth.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, brahmi roots have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce gum inflammation and prevent further damage.
Barks: Containing a variety of polyphenolic compounds, brahmi barks have antibacterial properties that aid in the fight against periodontal pathogens and promote healthy gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh bacopa monnieri leaves and stems from a trusted source.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 12 hours.
Grind the dried bacopa monnieri into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 20g of the powdered bacopa monnieri with 80g of potato starch and 10g of magnesium stearate in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared mixture, making sure each capsule contains an exact dose of 500mg.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry capsules helps with periodontal disease because they contain bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds, such as anthocyanins and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation in the gums, which is a key factor in the development and progression of periodontal disease. Additionally, the antioxidants present in blueberry capsules can help to prevent tissue damage and promote healthy cell growth, further supporting gum health and reducing the risk of complications such as tooth loss and bad breath.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and inflammation in the mouth, which can contribute to periodontal disease.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling and alleviate symptoms associated with gum disease, such as pain and bleeding.
Pectin: The soluble fiber content in blueberries may help promote healthy gums by reducing the formation of plaque and tartar, which can lead to periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help reduce inflammation and prevent bone loss associated with periodontal disease.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, the barks have antibacterial properties that combat harmful bacteria causing gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather and clean 500g of vaccinium myrtillus berries dried or fresh.
Grind 250g of the berries into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar pestle.
Mix the ground berry powder with 100g of organic rice flour and 50g of silica gel.
Encapsulate the mixture in vegetarian capsules in a sterile environment and store at room temperature for two weeks before consumption.
Label and package the finished herbal capsules containing 500mg per serving for periodontal disease prevention.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Alpine aster capsules helps with periodontal disease because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that have potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These capsules help to reduce the severity of gum inflammation, combat oral bacteria, and promote healing in the affected areas. By targeting the root causes of periodontal disease, alpine aster capsules can help to prevent further tissue damage and bone loss, ultimately leading to a healthier and more stable mouth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Alpine Aster capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes, which help reduce inflammation in the gums and surrounding tissues, thereby reducing the risk of periodontal disease.

Antioxidant activity: The capsules have antioxidant properties that help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of periodontal disease.
Immunomodulatory effects: Alpine Aster capsules contain immunomodulatory compounds that help modulate the immune response, reducing inflammation and preventing the progression of periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help combat oral bacteria and reduce inflammation associated with periodontal disease.
Rhizomes: Contain anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe gum inflammation and prevent further damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aster alpinus flowers and dry them using shade drying or air circulation for two weeks.
Grind dried aster alpinus flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for at least 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered aster alpinus with 500 milligrams of vitamin c powder in a small bowl.
Fill empty capsules with 250 milligrams of the mixed powder and seal them immediately using a capsule machine.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn capsules helps with periodontal disease because they contain bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. These compounds, such as flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, work to reduce inflammation in the gums and prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause infection. Additionally, hawthorn's antioxidant activity helps to protect gum tissue from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall oral health and reducing the risk of periodontal disease progression.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Hawthorn's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and anthraquinones, can reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with periodontal disease.
Antioxidant properties: Hawthorn's high antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that damage tissues and contribute to the progression of periodontal disease.
Immunomodulatory effects: Hawthorn's immunomodulating compounds may help regulate the immune response, reducing the severity of periodontal inflammation and promoting a healthier oral environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and anthocyanins, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with periodontal disease.
Barks: Containing a variety of bioactive compounds like phenolic acids and tannins, which exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties to combat gum disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried hawthorn berries from the crataegus monogyna plant.
Grind 30 grams of dried hawthorn berries into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered hawthorn with 50 grams of acacia fiber and 20 grams of vitamin c powder in a bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with a mixture of 500 milligrams of the herbal blend and 1000 milligrams of water, allowing for gentle pressing to form tight seals.
Store the finished herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silver daisy capsules helps with periodontal disease because they contain a powerful combination of herbs that work synergistically to reduce inflammation, combat bacteria, and promote healing. The silver in the capsule has antibacterial properties, helping to eliminate the underlying cause of periodontitis, while the herbal extracts soothe and calm the gums, reducing bleeding and swelling. Additionally, the capsules' anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with periodontal disease, promoting a healthy and balanced mouth.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Silver Daisy (Laggera alata) has antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the mouth, which are major contributors to periodontal disease.
Anti-inflammatory effect: The capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and pain associated with periodontitis by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.

Antimicrobial activity: Silver Daisy has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties against various bacteria, including those that can contribute to periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the silver daisy plant contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and bacteria growth, making them effective in treating periodontal disease.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their ability to stimulate the immune system, which helps fight off infection and promote healing in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ursinia anthemoides leaves measure out 200 grams.
Dry the leaves in a hot air circulation dryer at 40°c for 3 hours.
Grind the dried ursinia anthemoides into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 30 grams of the powdered ursinia anthemoides with 70 grams of potato starch capsules filling material.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture and store in an airtight container.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for periodontal disease are natural supplements containing potent herbal extracts that help combat periodontal disease, a bacterial infection that causes inflammation and damage to the gums and teeth. The best herbal capsules for periodontal disease include Curcuma longa, Melaleuca alternifolia, Echinacea purpurea, Ginkgo biloba, Sanguinaria canadensis, Salvia officinalis, Silybum marianum, Zingiber officinale, Trifolium pratense, and Eucalyptus globulus. If left untreated, periodontal disease can lead to tooth loss, bone damage, and even heart problems, significantly impacting one's quality of life, appearance, and overall health.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to periodontal disease that are treated with herbal capsules are various oral health concerns, including gum inflammation (gingivitis), tooth decay, and mouth ulcers. Herbal remedies like tea tree oil, clove oil, and turmeric have antibacterial properties that help combat bacterial growth, reduce inflammation, and promote healing in the gums and mouth tissues. Other herbs used to treat these conditions include garlic, ginger, and Echinacea.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with periodontal disease is a blend of Triphala, Neem, and Turmeric. Triphala's antimicrobial properties help reduce bacteria in the mouth, while Neem's anti-inflammatory effects soothe and calm gums. Turmeric, with its high curcumin content, reduces inflammation and fights off infections. Together, these three herbs promote healthy gum tissue and prevent the progression of periodontal disease. Regular consumption of these capsules can lead to improved gum health and a reduction in symptoms associated with periodontal disease.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Iranian journal of basic medical sciences, various herbal products have shown potential in managing periodontal diseases, including gingivitis. The study reviewed clinical trials on the effectiveness of herbal products in gingivitis, which included assessments of herbal medicines in the form of dentifrice, mouth rinse, gel, and gum, and found that pomegranate, aloe, green tea, and miswak, as well as some polyherbal formulations such as triphala, were significantly effective in managing gingivitis complications. These herbal products can act through several mechanisms, including a decrease in gingival inflammation and bleeding, inhibition of dental plaque formation, and improvement in different indices of oral hygiene, making them a good option for managing periodontal disease.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with periodontal disease because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums. By inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, curcumin helps to prevent the destruction of periodontal tissues and promote healing. Additionally, curcumin's antimicrobial properties help to combat the growth of bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease, creating a favorable environment for gum health and potentially reducing the risk of tooth loss.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: They help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in periodontal tissue, thereby alleviating symptoms of periodontal disease.
Demethoxycurcumin: It has antimicrobial properties that help control the growth of bacteria responsible for causing periodontal disease.
Tumerone: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect periodontal tissue from damage caused by inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Curcuma longa for periodontal disease treatment due to their high content of curcumin, a bioactive compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are also widely used for periodontal disease treatment as they contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including curcumin, that help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth.

Leaves: Leaves of Curcuma longa are sometimes used to make capsules for periodontal disease treatment due to their content of various bioactive compounds, including curcumin and other flavonoids, that help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500 grams of dried curcuma longa roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the powder with 500 grams of cornstarch and 200 grams of vegetable cellulose in a large bowl.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill 1000 vegetarian capsules with the mixture for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.
Label the container with the name of the herb, dosage, and preparation instructions for safe consumption.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia capsules helps with periodontal disease because they contain tea tree oil, a natural antimicrobial agent that inhibits the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms responsible for periodontal disease. The oil's antiseptic properties help reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of plaque, a major contributor to gum disease. Additionally, tea tree oil has been shown to promote wound healing and reduce oxidative stress, which can help repair damaged gum tissue and prevent further deterioration.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps with periodontal disease by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, thereby promoting a healthy oral environment.
Linalool: Contributes to the antimicrobial properties of Melaleuca alternifolia, helping to combat the bacterial growth that causes periodontal disease.
Caryophyllene oxide: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of periodontal disease by minimizing inflammation and promoting tissue healing.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These contain the active compound, cineole, which is effective in reducing inflammation and fighting bacteria that cause periodontal disease.
Rhyzomes: They contain the same active compound, cineole, as the leaves and are used to make capsules that help in treating periodontal disease.

Seeds: Although less commonly mentioned, some sources suggest that the seeds of Melaleuca alternifolia may also be used to make capsules for periodontal disease due to their potential antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves and flowers from a reputable supplier.
Dry the leaves and flowers in a low temperature oven at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 200 grams of the dried leaves and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 gram of the powder with 5 grams of gelatin and 5 milliliters of glycerin in a bowl.
Encapsulate the mixture in 100 empty vegetarian capsules using a manual capsule filler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with periodontal disease because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that combat the bacterial infections causing the condition. The herb's immunomodulatory effects help regulate the immune system's response to inflammation, preventing it from causing further damage to the gums and teeth. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress, which is often associated with periodontal disease. This dual action makes Echinacea purpurea a potentially effective natural remedy for periodontal disease prevention and management.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: It helps by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, which can contribute to the reduction of periodontal disease symptoms.
Caffeic acid: It helps by exhibiting antimicrobial properties that can inhibit the growth of bacteria associated with periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis.
Quercetin: It helps by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can reduce the severity of periodontal disease and promote healthy gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea purpurea are used to make capsules for periodontal disease because they contain high levels of alkaloids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in periodontal disease.

Roots (again) and Flowers: I have included roots again because the flowers also contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and in combination with the roots, can provide a synergistic effect in treating periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1000g of fresh echinacea purpurea roots and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the roots in a low-temperature oven at 50c for 8 hours or using a food dehydrator.
Grind 200g of dried echinacea purpurea root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Fill 100 empty vegetable capsules with 0.5g of echinacea purpurea root powder each using a capsule filler.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years to preserve potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with periodontal disease because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The capsules also enhance blood circulation, which helps to deliver oxygen and nutrients to the affected areas. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba has been shown to have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacteria and other pathogens that contribute to periodontal disease. By improving gum health and reducing inflammation, Ginkgo biloba capsules can aid in the prevention and treatment of periodontal disease.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation in the gums, thereby alleviating symptoms of periodontal disease such as redness and swelling.
Bilobalide: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of periodontitis by minimizing tissue damage and promoting healing.
Quercetin: As a potent antioxidant, quercetin can help protect gum tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, contributing to the management of periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help in reducing periodontal disease symptoms.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including periodontal disease, due to their flavonoid content, which has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects.
Leaves: They are also used for their antioxidant properties, which help in reducing inflammation and preventing oxidative stress, thereby contributing to the management of periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and powder them in a food processor for 30 seconds.
Mix 10 grams of ginkgo biloba powder with 10 grams of gelatin and 5 grams of glycerin in a bowl.
Add 50 milliliters of distilled water to the bowl and stir the mixture until the gelatin dissolves completely.
Pour the mixture into a capsule filling machine and fill 60 capsules at 0.5 grams each.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sanguinaria canadensis capsules helps with periodontal disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, also known as bloodroot, contains a compound called sanguinarine, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria associated with periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis. Additionally, sanguinarine has been found to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, promoting a healthy environment for tissue repair and regeneration, ultimately aiding in the prevention and treatment of periodontal disease. This makes it a promising natural remedy for oral health.
remedy_constituents: Protodaphniphylline: This alkaloid constituent helps with periodontal disease by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation and swelling associated with gum disease.
Isoquinoline alkaloids: These alkaloids help with periodontal disease by displaying antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria responsible for causing gum infections.
Chelidonine: This isoquinoline alkaloid constituent helps with periodontal disease by possessing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that protect gum tissue from damage and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Sanguinaria canadensis are used due to their alkaloid content, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions.
Leaves: The leaves of Sanguinaria canadensis contain alkaloids that have been used to treat different health conditions, including those related to the respiratory and nervous systems.

Roots: The roots of Sanguinaria canadensis are also used due to their sanguinarine content, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried root tubers of sanguinaria canadensis, ensuring they are free from contaminants and moisture.
Crush the dried root tubers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, taking about 30 minutes.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powdered sanguinaria canadensis root with 500 milligrams of cellulose powder, a carrier agent.
Fill gelatin capsules with the sanguinaria canadensis mixture, taking about 15 minutes to fill 100 capsules.
Store the herbal sanguinaria canadensis capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight and moisture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis capsules helps with periodontal disease because they are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as carnosic acid and ursolic acid. These compounds have been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, which can lead to periodontal disease. Additionally, Salvia officinalis has antimicrobial properties that can help eliminate existing bacterial infections, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing the risk of further periodontal damage. This makes Salvia officinalis a potential natural remedy for periodontal disease prevention and treatment.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: This triterpenoid helps with periodontal disease by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to gum disease progression.

Salvianolic acid A: As a phenolic compound, salvianolic acid A helps combat periodontal disease by exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, thereby reducing the severity of gum inflammation.
Carnosic acid: With its antioxidant properties, carnosic acid helps protect against periodontal disease by reducing the formation of free radicals and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds, which help combat periodontal disease.
Seeds: They contain oils with anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation associated with periodontal disease.
Buds: They are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties that can aid in periodontal health.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves from a reputable source and store them in an airtight container.
Weigh out 300 grams of the dried leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the ground powder with 200 grams of gelatin powder and 100 grams of tapioca starch in a large bowl.
Add 500 milliliters of distilled water to the bowl and stir the mixture with a wooden spoon for 5 minutes.
Use a capsule machine to fill 500 empty capsules with 0.5 grams of the herbal mixture each.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum capsules helps with periodontal disease because they contain the milk thistle herb, which has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, promoting healthy tissue regeneration and preventing further damage. The flavonoids present in milk thistle, such as silymarin, also exhibit antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease. This leads to improved gum health and reduced risk of complications.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: This flavonoid complex has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the gums and periodontal tissues.
Flavonoids (specifically isosilybin): These compounds have antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals, prevent the destruction of periodontal tissues, and promote healing.
Lignans (specifically silibinin): This lignan has antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal disease, reducing the severity of the condition.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and prevent further damage to gums and teeth.
Leaves: They are used due to their antioxidant properties, which help protect the gums and teeth from free radicals and oxidative stress.
Flowers: They are used due to their antimicrobial properties, which help eliminate bacteria and other pathogens that can cause periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest silybum marianum flowers and seeds from mature plants at the end of the summer season for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 100°f for 2 hours to prevent degradation.
Grind the dried silybum marianum into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill for uniform texture.
Mix 500mg of the silybum marianum powder with 50mg of a filler material such as cellulose or rice flour in a ratio of 10:1.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal them immediately to preserve the herbal extract's potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with periodontal disease because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compound gingerol in the capsules has been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that contribute to periodontal disease. Additionally, ginger has been found to stimulate blood flow and reduce oxidative stress in the gums, promoting healing and tissue regeneration. By targeting multiple aspects of periodontal disease, Zingiber officinale capsules provide a natural and holistic approach to managing the condition. Regular use may help alleviate symptoms and prevent progression.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent tissue damage associated with periodontal disease.
Shogaols: Shogaols have antimicrobial properties that help combat the growth of bacteria responsible for periodontal disease, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis.
Curcuminoids: These compounds have potent antioxidant properties that help prevent oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, thereby reducing the risk of periodontal disease progression.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots are also used due to their rich content of antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth in the gums.
Barks: Barks may be used due to their content of compounds with antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off bacteria that cause periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500g of dried zingiber officinale root into fine powder using a mill or grinder.
Mix 250g of the zingiber officinale powder with 250g of tapioca starch in a bowl.
Combine the starch and zingiber officinale mixture with 50g of gelatin in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring constantly, until the gelatin dissolves.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the cooled mixture, using a small spoon or scoop.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense capsules helps with periodontal disease because of their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herbs found in Trifolium pratense have been shown to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, which are key factors in the development and progression of periodontal disease. By inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and reducing inflammation, Trifolium pratense capsules can help to prevent further damage to the gums and teeth, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing the risk of complications associated with periodontal disease.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: Isoflavones have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the gums and teeth associated with periodontal disease.
Saponins: Saponins have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the bacteria that cause periodontal disease, reducing the severity of the condition.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the gums and teeth from oxidative stress, reducing the risk of periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots of Trifolium pratense are used due to their isoflavone content, particularly isoflavones like formononetin and genistein, which have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for periodontal disease.
Seeds: Seeds of Trifolium pratense are used for their isoflavone content, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to combat periodontal disease.

Flowers: Flowers of Trifolium pratense are used for their isoflavone content, particularly isoflavones like isoflavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties beneficial for periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of trifolium pratense flowers and leaves from a clean environment to ensure quality.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind the dried trifolium pratense into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 milligrams of the trifolium pratense powder with 100 milligrams of cellulose powder to create the capsule mixture.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus capsules helps with periodontal disease because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The capsules contain Eucalyptus globulus oil, which has been shown to reduce plaque and gingivitis, key contributors to periodontal disease. Additionally, its antibacterial properties help combat the bacteria that cause periodontal disease, promoting a healthy oral environment. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Eucalyptus globulus capsules can aid in the management and prevention of periodontal disease, promoting overall oral health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This terpene has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of periodontal disease by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause inflammation in the gums.
Eugenol: This phenolic compound has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help combat the bacterial infection and inflammation associated with periodontal disease.

β-Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent damage to the gums and bone structure associated with periodontal disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are a major source of eucalyptol, a key compound responsible for the plant's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The bark contains tannins and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that cause periodontal disease.
Leaves: The leaves are also a rich source of flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect gums and teeth from damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves from a trusted supplier or locally sourced plant.
Dry the leaves for 2 hours at 40°c to remove excess moisture and ensure potency.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250mg of the eucalyptus globulus powder using a capsule filler.
Label and package the capsules in airtight containers for storage and distribution within 30 minutes.
